source: NHTSA vPIC motorcycle models + curated aliases; fairing R&R hours are quoting heuristics
laborNote: fairingRrHours = estimated uninstall+reinstall of bodywork around a wrap (not film install time). Starting bands only — refine with photos and timed jobs.

naked_minimal.label: Naked / cafe — little to no bodywork
naked_minimal.notes: Tank focus; mirrors/light surrounds only. Fastest R&R.

cruiser_simple.label: Cruiser / bobber — tank + fenders
cruiser_simple.notes: Tank, front/rear fenders, side covers. Few fasteners; watch tank vent/petcock.

half_faired.label: Half fairing / sport-touring
half_faired.notes: Headlight cowl or belly pan + tank. More clips than a naked; less than a full race fairing.

adventure_touring.label: Adventure / dual-sport panels
adventure_touring.notes: Radiator shrouds, beak, tank plastics, side panels. Brittle aging plastics common.

full_sport.label: Full fairing sportbike
full_sport.notes: Upper/lower fairings, side panels, tank, seat cowl, often ram-air ducts.

touring_complex.label: Touring / bagger — complex plastics
touring_complex.notes: Fairing, lowers, bags, trunk interfaces. Plan a full day for R&R alone on big baggers.

scooter_enclosed.label: Scooter / covered plastics
scooter_enclosed.notes: Many interlocking body panels; watch wiring and floorboard plastics.

dirt_mx.label: Dirt / MX plastics
dirt_mx.notes: Number plates, fenders, side panels. Fast; plastics flex differently than street fairings.

unknown.label: Unclassified — confirm panels from photos
unknown.notes: Heuristic miss. Ask for photos before locking labor.
